title: Astel Buffer Table
description: Binary buffer table for per-primitive channels (provenance, materials, region ids, deformation). Deliberately glTF-shaped (buffers -> bufferViews -> accessors) so the glTF exporter is a near-identity transform. All buffers are little-endian; bufferViews are 4-byte aligned. See manifest-v0.md section 4.

buffers.description: Binary blobs referenced by relative path.

uri.description: POSIX relative path to a .bin file inside the package. No absolute paths, no '..' traversal.

byte_length.description: Total length of the buffer in bytes.

byte_offset.description: Offset into the buffer in bytes. Must keep the view 4-byte aligned.

byte_length.description: Length of the view in bytes.

byte_stride.description: Stride in bytes for interleaved data; omit for tightly packed.

accessors.description: Typed views over bufferViews. Per-gaussian accessors must have count equal to the bound splat layer's gaussian count and are index-aligned to it (the i-th element corresponds to the i-th gaussian).

accessor.title: Accessor
accessor.description: A typed array view. component_type uses glTF GL enums extended with UNORM aliases for clarity.

component_type.description: Component type. GL enums: 5120=BYTE, 5121=UNSIGNED_BYTE, 5122=SHORT, 5123=UNSIGNED_SHORT, 5125=UNSIGNED_INT, 5126=FLOAT. String aliases UNORM8/UNORM16 denote normalized unsigned integer storage (decoded to [0,1]).

count.description: Number of elements. For per-gaussian channels, equals the bound layer's gaussian count.

normalized.description: If true, integer values are normalized to [0,1] (unsigned) or [-1,1] (signed) on read. Implied true for UNORM8/UNORM16 aliases.

quantization.description: Optional dequantization parameters when a coarse integer type encodes a wider range than plain normalization.

scale.description: Multiplier applied after reading the stored integer.
